subject: Project Management
title: David Anderson's Breakthrough
description: A quick, practical guide to David Anderson's Breakthrough.

hook.text: In 2004, David Anderson was managing a struggling team at Microsoft. He tried something new.

buildup.text: He applied Toyota's kanban principles to the software team: visualize, limit WIP, measure flow.

discovery.text: Delivery time dropped from 6 months to 5 weeks without adding staff or changing the org chart.

twist.text: Anderson didn't ask anyone to change roles or adopt new practices — he just made work visible.

climax.text: His book 'Kanban' launched the Kanban Method for knowledge work — a global movement.

punchline.text: He didn't change the people. He changed what they could see.

quiz.question: What did David Anderson achieve at Microsoft using Kanban?
options: Reduced delivery time from 6 months to 5 weeks
